theory.intro: Perimeter is the distance around a shape. When sides are given in **different units**, convert them to the same unit first, then add.
facts: Perimeter $=$ the sum of **all** side lengths.
Convert lengths to a common unit before adding: $1\text{ m} = 100\text{ cm}$, $1\text{ cm} = 10\text{ mm}$, $1\text{ km} = 1\,000\text{ m}$.
For a rectangle $P = 2 \times (\text{length} + \text{width})$; for a square $P = 4 \times \text{side}$.
steps: List every side length around the shape.
Convert all lengths to the same unit.
Add them and write the answer with the correct unit.
